messages.content: Write a forum conversation in November 2023 where Kids WB begins making some schedule changes, firstly they have gotten out of the E/I quota, so Will and Dewitt leaves 7am, is replaced with Teen Titans Go, TTG relinquishes it's slot at 08:00am to Xiaolin Showdown

**Thread Title**: Big Changes Coming to Kids WB Lineup in November 2023!

**ChaosController01**: Hey folks, have you heard the latest news? Kids WB is shaking things up in their schedule. Apparently, they’re no longer tied to the E/I content requirements, so they’re booting "Will and Dewitt" from the 7am slot.

**ToonFan4Ever**: Really? No more E/I early mornings? That’s a shocker. So what’s taking its place?

**ChaosController01**: "Teen Titans Go!" will be filling that 7am slot. I guess they’re trying to pull in more of the early riser crowd.

**ToonFan4Ever**: And where's TTG moving from then?

**OtakuKid92**: It’s moving from the 8am spot. I saw on their Twitter that "Xiaolin Showdown" is going to be taking over the 8am timeslot. I'm pretty excited to see how this change pans out.

**90sCartoonHead**: "Xiaolin Showdown" at 8am? That's a throwback! I wonder if the nostalgia factor will work for them.

**SaturdayMornNostalgia**: Does anyone know if Kids WB will replace "Will and Dewitt" with anything else or are they completely ditching the educational stuff?

**ChaosController01**: From what I can gather, they’re not replacing it directly with another E/I program. They seem to be rebranding their morning lineup to cater more to the action and humor-focused shows.

**CapeAndCowl**: Bold move Not satisfying the E/I quota could potentially alienate some parents, but it might attract more kids looking for entertainment instead of education early in the morning.

**MorningToonWatcher**: Tbh, I never really watched "Will and Dewitt" anyway. "Teen Titans Go!" is way more my speed for Saturday morning vibes. It's a smart move.

**CartoonEducator**: A little disappointed in the shift away from educational programming. E/I has its place, even if it’s not the biggest ratings draw. Some balance is important, right?

**ToonFan4Ever**: Well, it's a business at the end of the day. And let’s be honest, E/I shows haven’t been Kids WB’s forte for a while now.

**ChaosController01**: I think they’re banking on ratings and reviving interest in the network. It's been pretty stagnant lately.

**SuperSaturdayFan**: Ratings drive the decisions, I suppose. Either way, I'm pumped for "Xiaolin Showdown." Let's hope it doesn't stop there, I am looking forward to seeing what else might come up!

**90sCartoonHead**: Agreed, let’s see how this goes. Maybe it’ll be the start of a new golden era for Kids WB or maybe they’ll regret straying from the E/I content. Only time will tell.

**Thread Moderator**: A friendly reminder to keep the discussion civil and respectful, folks. We're all here for the love of cartoons!
Write that the only fun thing about the leaving show was that Dewitt was a shape-shifting frog, Other than that, it was mid, Something like KikoRiki would be more fun and fulfill E/I quotas

**CartoonEducator**: That's fair to say about "Will and Dewitt". The shape-shifting frog was a unique aspect, and it certainly had some entertaining moments for kids interested in that kind of creativity. But overall, the show was pretty mid-tier entertainment-wise.

**SuperSaturdayFan**: Right? Dewitt's antics were kinda the only fun twist. My cousin loved trying to guess what shape he'd turn into next. But besides that, it wasn't something you were rushing to wake up for on a Saturday morning.

**MorningToonWatcher**: I think if Kids WB wanted to keep an E/I show that's both fun and educational, they might look at something like "KikoRiki". That show is zany enough to keep kids laughing while still slipping in those learning moments. Plus, the characters are all so different, it's a bit like watching a cartoon version of a personality quiz, ha!

**90sCartoonHead**: Exactly! "KikoRiki" could be a good way to hit the E/I target and keep things entertaining. Those little critters get up to some wild adventures, and it's not just fun; there's plenty of moral lessons tied in organically.

**CapeAndCowl**: "KikoRiki" could be an excellent substitution indeed. It doesn't treat kids like they can't handle complex ideas, and that's the key. The characters are all so distinct and each episode packs a punch with humor and heart.

**ToonFan4Ever**: I agree, "KikoRiki" has the potential to fill the gap and provide that balance of fun and education. It’s got a subtle way of introducing concepts that don't feel heavy-handed or overly "teachy". Plus, on a personal note, I’d much appreciate the change from watching the same kind of E/I content Kids WB has been sticking to lately.

**SaturdayMornNostalgia**: It would be good for them to mix it up with something like "KikoRiki". As long as they can present it in a way that doesn't completely break away from their current vibe, it'd be a smart move to pull in the E/I crowd without losing their entertainment edge.

**Thread Moderator**: "KikoRiki" seems like a popular suggestion! Let's hope the network executives are browsing these forums and taking notes. You never know – fan feedback often plays a bigger part than we think! Keep the suggestions coming, everyone.
Write a follow up where the rights to Squish expire in late January, and it seems Scooby Doo:MI is ending in Feburary,

**Thread Title**: Squish Rights Expire & Scooby Doo: MI Wrapping Up?!

**ChaosController01**: Hey team, just saw the update on Kids WB's upcoming schedule changes. Looks like they're losing the rights to "Squish" by the end of January. Any of you going to miss it?

**90sCartoonHead**: "Squish" had its moments, but it wasn't the strongest show out there. Losing the rights might give them room to bring something more engaging to the lineup.

**ToonFan4Ever**: I guess it had a decent run. These things happen with the rights to shows. Kids WB seems to be going through a phase of rights expiration and show conclusions.

**MysteryGangMember**: More importantly, word is going around that "Scooby Doo: Mystery Incorporated" might have its series finale in February. If it's true, feels like the end of an era!

**SuperSaturdayFan**: Wait, what? "Scooby Doo: MI" is wrapping up? That’s been one of their strongest shows. Got any more deets on that?

**CapeAndCowl**: Yeah, I was reading up on some fansites and the news isn't official yet. But the voice actors have hinted at recording their last lines and there is talk about a series wrap-up party. Seems legit that it could be ending.

**MorningToonWatcher**: No way! SD:MI is the best incarnation of Scooby Doo in a long while. I'm genuinely sad about this. The story arcs, the character development—this show had it all.

**SaturdayMornNostalgia**: I've got mixed feelings about this. On one hand, it's sad to see such a quality show go. On the other, ending on a high note is better than dragging it out until it gets stale.

**CartoonEducator**: Agreed, it's what a classic deserves. Plus, it opens up speculation about what might fill their time slots. Any insights on replacement shows to take over from "Squish" and "Scooby Doo: MI"?

**ChaosController01**: Nothing solid on replacements yet. I reckon they might lean toward more action-oriented shows, considering their recent shifts with the schedule. Maybe something new? Or another classic reboot?

**ToonFan4Ever**: Considering the recent trends, I wouldn't be surprised to see a new franchise or a reboot indeed. Kids WB has been on a nostalgia trip lately, could be time for another classic to make a comeback.

**Thread Moderator**: As always, we'll be keeping an eye out for any official announcements from Kids WB. Discussions are great, but remember to take rumors with a grain of salt until we have confirmed information! Stay tuned, everyone!
Write a follow up where to the sigh of everyone, it returns, Everyone is "Not this show again, I thought it was gone for good"

**Thread Title**: Squish Back on the Schedule? Say It Isn't So...

**ChaosController01**: Brace yourselves. You're not gonna believe this... "Squish" is coming back to Kids WB. I repeat, "Squish" is making a return. 😐

**90sCartoonHead**: Wait, what?! I thought we were done with that. How did it make a comeback? I thought the rights expired!

**ToonFan4Ever**: Ugh, my little bro just confirmed it. He was on the Kids WB site and there it was - "Squish" on the Saturday morning lineup. 🙄 I seriously thought we had moved past this show...

**MysteryGangMember**: This is like that one villain in "Scooby Doo" who keeps escaping and coming back for another episode. When will we get the new, cool content we were promised?

**SuperSaturdayFan**: Noooo! I wasn't a fan the first time round. It felt like filler then, and it feels even more like filler now. Kids WB, what are you doing?

**CapeAndCowl**: I'm just as stumped as you guys are. After all the talk of shaking things up and bringing in classics like "Xiaolin Showdown", they go and bring back... "Squish"? 🤨

**MorningToonWatcher**: I'm trying not to be too negative here, but "Squish"? Seriously? They must be in a serious content pinch to rehash this one.

**SaturdayMornNostalgia**: It's like they had a space to fill and just threw in anything they could find. I was hoping for something fresh or another underrated classic, not "Squish" x 2.

**CartoonEducator**: Hard to fathom the decision-making sometimes. It's not just nostalgia; it's almost... regression? Especially after freeing up time slots from "Squish" and "Scooby Doo: MI". Feels like a missed opportunity.

**ToonFan4Ever**: Has anyone considered that maybe the licensing deal was more complex than we thought? Maybe "Squish" had a bounce-back clause we didn't know about? Still, I'm disappointed.

**ChaosController01**: Anything's possible, but a bounce-back clause for "Squish" sure isn't something to celebrate. I'm all for giving shows a second chance, but this feels like a misstep.

**Thread Moderator**: Well, the community has spoken loud and clear! Let's keep an eye out for future changes—hopefully, Kids WB takes such feedback into account. Keep the conversation respectful, and let's stay hopeful for more exciting content down the line!

Write a follow up where they listen, and take onboard the E/I request made back when WAD left, The user says "It is so much better than both Will and Dewitt and Squish, Also, I hope they give it more reruns, maybe when SDMI ends in Feburary"

**Thread Title**: Kids WB Takes a Turn - New E/I Content Incoming!

**ChaosController01**: Big update, everyone! Looks like Kids WB has been keeping an ear to the ground. They're introducing a new E/I show to the block — and it's actually good!

**90sCartoonHead**: Really?! That's great news. Do you have any details on what the show is about?

**ChaosController01**: No specifics yet, but the buzz is it's both fun and educational. Way better than "Will and Dewitt" and certainly a step up from "Squish". 

**ToonFan4Ever**: It's about time! This is what we've been asking for. Quality E/I content that doesn't make you want to hit the snooze button.

**SuperSaturdayFan**: Now that's a development I can get behind! I hope they give it a solid spot. Maybe inherit some of those "Scooby Doo: MI" times when it wraps up in February?

**MorningToonWatcher**: That would be perfect. The post-"SD:MI" slot is prime real estate. I hope this new E/I show gets the exposure it deserves, especially if it’s as good as you say.

**CapeAndCowl**: Finally, a change we can all applaud. With the right show, they could really knock it out of the park.

**SaturdayMornNostalgia**: Thumbs up here! I'm all for something that can entertain and teach at the same time. Out with the mid-tier; in with the quality!

**CartoonEducator**: Echoing the positive sentiments here. A well-crafted E/I show caters not just to kids but also to parents looking for engaging content for their children. Hopefully, they’ll consider giving it ample rerun times throughout the week too.

**ToonFan4Ever**: Yes to reruns! Spread the goodness around. Give kids and parents more chances to catch it, not just the early birds.

**ChaosController01**: I'm just relieved they're taking our feedback seriously. This could be the start of a new chapter for Kids WB, and I'm here for it.

**Thread Moderator**: The anticipation for the new E/I show is building, and the time slot post-"SD:MI" would indeed be ideal. Let's keep the conversation going and maybe, just maybe, Kids WB is tuning in right here! Remember to be vocal but kind in your discussions. And stay tooned for more updates!

Write a follow up where ONE hint is dropped "It's about cute animals" and the user immediately guesses correctly, even saying "I suggested it back when WAD was removed" The OP says "For guessing correctly, you win a gift bag"

**Thread Title**: New E/I Show Teaser: "It's About Cute Animals"

**ChaosController01**: Just got a teaser about the new E/I show coming to Kids WB, folks. All they said was, "It's about cute animals." Any guesses?

**MorningToonWatcher**: That's pretty vague. Cute animals? That could be anything from "Paw Patrol" to "Peppa Pig". We need more clues!

**SuperSaturdayFan**: I'm thinking along the lines of "My Little Pony" territory with that hint. But that's been done many times before.

**ToonFan4Ever**: Hang on... cute animals, E/I... Guys, could it be "KikoRiki"? I remember suggesting it back when "Will and Dewitt" was removed! That show is all about quirky and cute animals learning life lessons — would be a perfect fit!

**90sCartoonHead**: "KikoRiki"? Yeah, I remember that conversation. It'd be a surprising choice, but a welcome one. Still, that's just one guess out of who knows how many 'cute animal' shows.

**ChaosController01**: Ding, ding, ding! We have a winner! Just got word that "ToonFan4Ever" nailed it — "KikoRiki" it is! Looks like your suggestion made it through the ranks, or you're just that good at guessing. ;)

**ToonFan4Ever**: No way! Seriously?! That's awesome! 😄 I'm so stoked! "KikoRiki" really is a fun show, and it's got the right mix of entertainment and education. Can't wait to see it on the schedule.

**ChaosController01**: As a little thank you for your spot-on guess (and maybe for the nudge you might've given the network), Kids WB is sending a "KikoRiki" gift bag your way! Expect some merchandise and maybe even exclusive sneak peeks at the new show!

**ToonFan4Ever**: Ahhh, thank you so much! I was just happy to discuss my favorite toons, never expected this! You guys rock, and so does Kids WB for actually listening!

**SaturdayMornNostalgia**: Way to go "ToonFan4Ever"! And it's really cool that "KikoRiki" is coming to Kids WB. I’m optimistic about this change — feels like Saturday mornings might be fun again!

**SuperSaturdayFan**: Lucky! Enjoy the swag, "ToonFan4Ever"! And, yep, gotta give props to Kids WB for shaking things up in a good way. Here's to hoping "KikoRiki" outshines both "Squish" and "Will and Dewitt"!

**Thread Moderator**: Fantastic guessing "ToonFan4Ever," and congratulations! It's encouraging seeing the network engage with the community like this. Keep the passion alive, everyone, and enjoy the show!
Write a announcement tweet saying "No more ameobas or shape-shifting frogs"

**Kids WB Official Twitter Account**

🎉 Big Announcement! 🎉

Say goodbye to ameobas and shape-shifting frogs! 🦠🐸 We're refreshing our lineup with something new and exciting that we know you're going to adore! 🌟🚀 Stay tuned for more updates and get ready for a wild ride with our upcoming shows! #KidWB #SaturdayMorningFun
Write a follow up going into more detail about the changeover to the new show

**Kids WB Official Twitter Account**

📺 It's Time for an Epic Schedule Shake-Up! 🎈👀

We took your feedback to heart and waved farewell to our single-celled friends and our amphibian magician 🚫🦠🐸. Stepping into the spotlight is none other than... "KikoRiki"! 🐰🎉

Get ready for a whirlwind of fun, laughter, and learning with a crew of adorable, round animals that each embody unique personalities and life lessons. "KikoRiki" perfectly blends entertainment with educational messages that adults love and kids can't get enough of!

✅ More reruns? You got it!
✅ E/I content that doesn't feel like homework? Absolutely!
✅ "KikoRiki" themed giveaways? Stay tuned...

Prepare for a heartfelt mix of storytelling and character development that will captivate and educate. The adventure begins this February, after a fond farewell to "Scooby Doo: MI" 👻🕵️.

Bookmark our schedule 🗓️, set your alarms ⏰, and get the whole family ready for a brand-new journey in Kids WB's world of wonder and excitement! 🎢🌍

#NewBeginnings #KikoRiki #EducationalFun #KidsWB

Write a forum conversation where the final countdown begins, the Evil Entity arc begins on Scooby Doo, This arc lasts for 3 episodes so we only have 4 more weeks to wait, Also the user is counting down the daily untill S and WAD is gone for good

**Thread Title**: The Final Countdown: Evil Entity Arc Incoming & Kiss Squish and W&D Goodbye!

**MysteryGangLeader**: Can you believe it's finally happening? The Evil Entity arc on Scooby Doo: MI is beginning this weekend. Just three epic episodes left in the saga! 🕵️‍♂️💥

**ClockIsTicking**: It's bittersweet, but I'm counting down the days! 🗓️ Only four weeks left to say our farewells. And not-so-secretly, I’m also ticking off the days until Squish and Will and Dewitt are officially off the air for good. The countdown is real, folks!

**SaturdaySleuth**: This is it! The culmination of all the cryptic messages and spooky build-up! The Evil Entity storyline is one for the ages. What a way to close out a legendary series. 💔 But hey, on the bright side, new adventures await with "KikoRiki."

**CountdownCrusader**: Daily Reminder: Just 28 days until "Squish" makes its last squelch and "Will and Dewitt" hops away! 🐸 #TheFinalCountdown

**ToonFan4Ever**: I'm feeling that countdown energy. It's like the end of an era with SD:MI, but also like the morning cartoon landscape is about to get a much-needed refresh. "KikoRiki" can't come soon enough! 🔄

**NostalgiaKnight**: Only three episodes to unravel the mysteries of the Evil Entity. My heart is ready but also not ready. And I share that energy with CountdownCrusader — Bring on the new content! 👋

**AnimationAddict**: The anticipation for the Evil Entity arc finale is real! Can't wait to see how everything ties together. It’s truly an end of an era with SD:MI. But honestly, I won't shed a tear for "Squish" or "Will and Dewitt." Ready for the fresh start! 🎬🌟

**ClockIsTicking**: **Day 21**: Post-Evil Entity, the countdown continues! The anticipation for the new lineup is actually giving me life right now. Hang tight, folks — change is coming! ⌛️

**ToonTimewarp**: Evil Entity arc is what Saturday mornings were made for, and those final episodes are going to be intense! Yet, there's something refreshing about moving on from the old. I, for one, welcome our new "KikoRiki" overlords 😆

**ClockIsTicking**: **Day 14**: Two weeks left until we dive into a new era of Kids WB! The Evil Entity will soon be confined to the annals of cartoon history, and we'll have a fresh start with no more shapeshifting or amoebas. 😌✌️

**ChillToonWatcher**: I'm half in mourning for SD:MI and half celebrating the future without Squish's stretch or Dewitt's hops. But that's the beauty of cartoons — they never really leave us. They live on in reruns and in our hearts. ❤️📺

**ClockIsTicking**: **Final Week Alert**: This is not a drill! 🚨 One more week until "Will and Dewitt" and "Squish" make their exit, and a new chapter begins with "KikoRiki." The countdown’s almost over. I'm so ready for this! 🎊

**Thread Moderator**: Let's keep that excitement going and remember to celebrate the great shows we've had as well as the ones to come. Only a week to go until the new programming starts! Prepare for the shift, and happy viewing everyone! 🎉📺

Write a follow up when KR finally debuts and everyone says "Krash is far better than Squish or Dewitt ever were, Even with the latter's shape-shifting gimmick" They also say that "Out of all the childrens books in the world, why Squish, Why couldn't it have been CATCF or DOWAK, But anyways a far better show has took its place, But I do miss Scooby already"

**Thread Title**: KikoRiki Debut: Krash Over Squish, Dewitt Ditched for the Better!

**ToonFan4Ever**: It finally happened!! "KikoRiki" debuted this weekend and let me tell you, Krash is a blast — way more fun than "Squish" or "Will and Dewitt" combined. 🎉 And I never thought I'd say this, but I don't even miss Dewitt's shape-shifting!

**NostalgiaKnight**: Totally agree! Krash's energy is what Saturday mornings needed. It feels like the Kids WB slate has been revived. 🐑💥 But I've gotta say, the void left by "Scooby Doo: MI" is definitely felt. Miss those Mystery Inc. vibes.

**BookishToon**: About "Squish", right? Out of all the children's books to adapt, "Squish" was... an interesting choice. Who wasn't dreaming of a "Charlie and the Chocolate Factory" series, or something from "Diary of a Wimpy Kid"? But, hey, "KikoRiki" has taken the baton and is sprinting with it!

**SaturdaySleuth**: It's like Kids WB finally hit the target with "KikoRiki". As for "Scooby", it'll always be close to our hearts. Feels odd not having the gang around, but nostalgia can't beat quality content like "KikoRiki" is delivering.

**ChillToonWatcher**: I'm with you all. Krash's antics are a total win. Who would've thought we could get something so educational without sacrificing fun? And on the "Scooby Doo" front, I'm sure it'll be back in some form or another. The gang's too iconic to stay away for long.

**AnimationAddict**: I was skeptical at first, I admit. But "KikoRiki" won me over instantly! Krash is definitely the star of the show. Now it feels like we're in a new golden age of Kids WB shows. And while "Scooby" leaves big shoes to fill, I'm confident we're headed in the right direction.

**ToonTimewarp**: Honestly, the energy in "KikoRiki" is contagious! Makes me wonder why we ever settled for "Squish" or the one-trick pony, Dewitt. A fresh start was overdue. But "Scooby Doo" reruns will keep me company when I start missing the gang. 

**ClockIsTicking**: Krash has crash-landed into my favorites list! 🚀 I thought I'd miss "Scooby" more, but "KikoRiki" is filling that cartoon-shaped hole in my Saturday. I'm sure we'll get another "Scooby" series down the line — it's as inevitable as the next sunrise.

**BookishToon**: Plus, if we're talking book adaptations, "KikoRiki" shows that translating engaging stories into cartoons can be done right. Educational, entertaining, and no gooey amoebas needed. 😂

**Thread Moderator**: "KikoRiki" has certainly made an impression, and it's great to see so much enthusiasm for the new addition to the Kids WB family. While we reminisce about the shows we've said goodbye to, let's also enjoy the new memories we're creating with shows like "KikoRiki". Keep enjoying the cartoons and sharing your thoughts, everyone!
Write a tweet where they think Squish, Will and Dewitt were killed  by the Kriegstaffebots (Pericles's henchmen in Scooby Doo), This is a jokey tweet BTW

**@ToonFan4Ever**

Breaking news! The mystery of what happened to #Squish, Will, and Dewitt has been solved by our very own Mystery Gang! They were last seen being chased off the schedule by the infamous Kriegstaffebots. Looks like #ScoobyDoo villains are doing us a solid! 🐶🤖 #SaturdayMorningSwitchUp #JustForLaughs
